task:
ATOM 8a — Make the Play contract real, not stubbed (the foundation the real-path proof harness in 8b will exercise). Three cohesive, unit-testable changes in packages/core and packages/personas. Do NOT build the proof harness in this atom (that is the next atom); this atom makes the convention/validator/hybrid-Play real and proves them with unit tests + typecheck.

CONTEXT (verified current state):
- packages/core/src/plays/types.ts defines: PlayDeterministicStep = { ref: string }, PlayOutputValidator = { ref: string }, and the Play interface (executionModel 'prompt-only'|'hybrid', triggerClass, outputValidator, deterministicStep, commitMode, etc.). These types are DONE — do not change the shape unless strictly necessary; if you must, keep it additive and backward-compatible.
- packages/core/src/plays/dispatch.ts: dispatchPlay runs deterministicStep via runDeterministicProcess(), which treats input.ref as a BARE EXECUTABLE command (no args). The comment at the top of runDeterministicProcess explicitly says 'Atom 8 owns the durable ref->script convention; for now the ref is treated as an executable command'. On deterministic failure it gates (gated:true, exitCode 1, no LLM). On success it feeds output into the prompt under '## Deterministic precheck result'.
- packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts validates wrap-up output via a BESPOKE hardcoded path: founderCloseoutFormatIssues() / parseFounderCloseoutContract() (~lines 245-303, used ~1118-1126). The wrap-up Play (packages/personas/base/plays/wrap-up.md) does NOT declare an outputValidator field today.
- No shipped Play currently uses deterministicStep (only test fixtures do). code-review.md is executionModel: prompt-only.

MAKE THESE THREE CHANGES:

(1) FINALIZE the deterministicStep ref->command convention in packages/core/src/plays/dispatch.ts. Replace the 'minimal default' with a durable, documented resolution: a ref is a repo-root-relative path to a deterministic check script; if it ends in '.mjs' run it via the node executable (process.execPath), otherwise treat it as a directly-executable file resolved against the run cwd/repo root. Resolve the path safely (reject refs that escape the repo root). Keep the bounded-subprocess + timeout + AbortSignal behavior and the {ok, output} contract intact. Document the convention in a short doc comment AND add/extend unit tests in packages/core/tests/plays-dispatch.test.ts proving: (a) a real .mjs ref is run via node and its exit code maps to ok, (b) a non-.mjs executable ref runs directly, (c) a ref that escapes the repo root is rejected. Keep the existing injected-runDeterministic seam working for the other tests.

(2) WIRE the outputValidator field to DRIVE wrap-up validation — one owner, no second copy of the contract. Add an outputValidator: { ref: ... } to packages/personas/base/plays/wrap-up.md frontmatter, and change runner.ts so the closeout validation is SELECTED/DERIVED from the Play's declared outputValidator.ref instead of being hardcoded against the wrap-up Play unconditionally. The existing founderCloseoutFormatIssues logic should be REACHED THROUGH the declared ref (e.g. a small validator registry keyed by ref, or the ref resolving to the existing checker), NOT bypassed or duplicated. Behavior must be preserved exactly: the same closeout-format issues are still detected. A Play with no outputValidator declared keeps today's behavior (no output validation). Add a unit test proving validation is driven by the declared ref (e.g. removing/omitting the field disables it; the wrap-up Play's ref selects the founder-closeout checker).

(3) MIGRATE ONE real Play to hybrid with a real scripts/* deterministic step. Convert packages/personas/base/plays/code-review.md to executionModel: hybrid with deterministicStep: { ref: '<scripts path>' }, and CREATE that real deterministic script under scripts/ (e.g. scripts/checks/code-review-preflight.mjs) that performs a genuine cheap precheck (e.g. verifies there is a non-empty diff / changed files to review and exits non-zero with a clear message when there is nothing to review, so it can demonstrably GATE). The script must be runnable standalone (node scripts/checks/<name>.mjs) and follow the ref->command convention from change (1). Add a unit/loader test proving code-review.md now parses as hybrid with the deterministicStep ref present.

MUST NOT BREAK (backward compatibility is part of the objective):
- All existing prompt-only Plays must still parse and dispatch unchanged.
- Existing plays-dispatch.test.ts, plays.test.ts, and runner.test.ts assertions must still pass (update them only where the convention/validator wiring legitimately changes expected behavior, and explain each change).
- The one-level dispatch boundary stands (no recursive Play delegation, no PlayAssignment[] multi-binding, no full Play-body injection).
- Do not change the Play type shape except additively if unavoidable.

WRITE-SCOPE: packages/core/src/plays/**, packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts, packages/core/tests/**, packages/personas/base/plays/code-review.md, packages/personas/base/plays/wrap-up.md, scripts/checks/** (new). Do not touch other personas, priorities, or unrelated runner logic.

EXIT CRITERION (scripted — run these and report exit codes + relevant output):
- pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test  (vitest run — all green, including your new tests)
- pnpm --filter @cocoder/core exec tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json  (typecheck clean)
- node scripts/checks/code-review-preflight.mjs  (runs standalone; show it exits non-zero when there is nothing to review and zero when there is — demonstrate the gate)
Report: the full diff, the three commands' exit codes and output, and a one-line note on how backward compatibility for prompt-only Plays is preserved. Do NOT run git.

ATOM 8b — Build the F18 real-path proof harness scripts/proof-hybrid-play.mjs that proves the Play system end-to-end through the REAL dispatch path (NOT mocked runners, NOT passing unit tests). This is the final atom of the priority. Atom 8a already landed the foundation (committed b497920): the deterministicStep ref->command convention is finalized in packages/core/src/plays/dispatch.ts; wrap-up output validation is driven by the declared outputValidator ref via the validators/founder-closeout registry in packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts; code-review.md is now executionModel: hybrid with deterministicStep scripts/checks/code-review-preflight.mjs (a real git-based gate). Your job is to PROVE these run for real, in one re-runnable command.

DELIVERABLE: a new script scripts/proof-hybrid-play.mjs, runnable as `node scripts/proof-hybrid-play.mjs`, following the structure and PASS/FAIL clause-table output style of the existing scripts/proof-*.mjs harnesses (e.g. proof-direct-spine.mjs, proof-plays.mjs). Reach @cocoder/core the same way those existing harnesses do (match their import/build/run mechanism — do not invent a new one). It must exit non-zero if any clause fails.

HARD CONSTRAINT — REAL PATH ONLY. The harness MUST exercise the production code paths: call the REAL exported dispatchPlay / runRun / loadPlay from @cocoder/core using their DEFAULT runners. It must NOT import or inject fakeRunHeadless, must NOT pass a custom runDeterministic, and must NOT mock the adapter. Green unit tests are necessary but NOT sufficient and do not satisfy this atom; runtime behavior must be observed, not inferred.

The harness must prove these clauses, each printed PASS/FAIL with the real evidence captured:

CLAUSE A — hybrid Play deterministic step is REAL and FEEDS the LLM: In a real temporary git repo with staged/changed files, load the real base code-review Play (loadPlay) and run the real dispatchPlay with its DEFAULT deterministic runner. Assert the real scripts/checks/code-review-preflight.mjs actually executed (its 'code-review preflight passed: N changed file(s)' output appears in the captured deterministic result), the deterministic output was injected into the LLM prompt under '## Deterministic precheck result', and a REAL LLM invocation occurred and produced captured output.

CLAUSE B — hybrid deterministic step GATES the LLM: In a real temporary git repo with NO changes to review, run the same real dispatchPlay path. Assert the preflight exits non-zero, dispatch returns gated:true / exitCode 1, and the LLM was NOT invoked (no adapter/headless call happened). This proves the deterministic result demonstrably BLOCKS the LLM layer.

CLAUSE C — mandatory trigger invokes wrap-up WITHOUT persona discretion AND validates output via the declared field: Drive the REAL runner wrap-up path (runRun, or the smallest real runner slice that reaches wrap-up dispatch) so the runner — not a persona choice — invokes the wrap-up Play, and the produced closeout is validated through the Play's declared outputValidator ref (validators/founder-closeout), reusing the wired field, NOT a bespoke local copy of the contract. Prove BOTH directions with real output: a well-formed closeout passes validation, and a malformed closeout is caught/flagged by the field-driven validator. A real LLM invocation must be part of the wrap-up dispatch path (do not hand-write the closeout in place of the model unless the model is genuinely unavailable — see adapter rule).

ADAPTER (real LLM) RULE: Select a real headless-capable adapter from the same adapter registry the runner uses (e.g. codex / claude / cursor-agent), configurable via an env var (e.g. COCODER_PROOF_ADAPTER) with a sensible default. If NO real adapter is available/authenticated in the environment, the harness must FAIL LOUDLY with a clear message naming exactly what to configure — it must NOT silently skip, mock, or stub the LLM. Bound every real invocation with a watchdog timeout and reject on missing output artifacts (no infinite polling).

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: do not modify the 8a foundation behavior (convention, validator wiring, hybrid Play) except tiny additive exports if the harness genuinely needs them — if you must export something new from @cocoder/core to reach a real path, keep it minimal, additive, and explain why. All existing tests must still pass. Do not weaken any test. One-level dispatch boundary stands.

WRITE-SCOPE: scripts/** (new harness, primary), and packages/core/src/** ONLY for minimal additive exports the harness provably needs (justify each). Do not touch personas, priorities, or unrelated runner logic.

EXIT CRITERION (run these and report exit codes + the FULL captured output, including the real LLM transcript/output and the deterministic step output):
- node scripts/proof-hybrid-play.mjs  (the harness itself — must exit 0 with every clause PASS, using a REAL adapter; paste the full clause table and the real captured outputs proving A/B/C)
- pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test  (still all green)
- pnpm --filter @cocoder/core exec tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json  (clean)
Report the full diff, the three commands' exit codes, and explicitly state which real adapter the harness used and the evidence that the LLM was actually invoked in A and C and actually gated (not invoked) in B. Do NOT run git. If a real adapter is genuinely unavailable in your environment, STOP and report that precisely (the exact adapter/auth missing) rather than mocking — that is a real result I need to see, not a reason to fake the proof.

ATOM 8b (REVISION) — Fix the proof harness scripts/proof-hybrid-play.mjs that was rejected. The harness already exists in the working tree (uncommitted). When I ran `node scripts/proof-hybrid-play.mjs` with the real codex adapter: Clause A (hybrid deterministic step feeds the LLM) PASSED with real evidence, and Clause B (deterministic failure gates the LLM, 0 adapter build calls) PASSED. DO NOT change clauses A and B — they are correct and proven. The harness exits 1 ONLY because Clause C's negative/rejection path is misdesigned.

ROOT CAUSE (verified by reading the captured output): Clause C's 'malformed' case instructs the real LLM to 'intentionally emit exactly this malformed closeout: PLAY CLOSEOUT'. A capable model (gpt-5.5, high reasoning) IGNORED that instruction and produced a fully well-formed Founder Completion Brief, so the field-driven validator CORRECTLY passed it as completed with no issues. You cannot prove a validator's rejection path by asking a real model to emit garbage — it won't reliably comply. The rejection must be proven DETERMINISTICALLY against the REAL production validation function, selected by the Play's declared outputValidator ref.

FIX — two parts:

(1) ADD ONE MINIMAL ADDITIVE EXPORT from @cocoder/core (this is the kind of minimal export the harness provably needs, allowed by scope): expose the runner's field-driven Play output validation as a public function — the SAME function the runner already uses internally in packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts (validatePlayOutput / the validators/founder-closeout registry path). ONE OWNER: refactor so the runner imports/uses the exported function; do NOT leave a private copy in runner.ts and a second copy elsewhere. The exported function must: take a Play + candidate output (+ cwd), select the validator by the Play's declared outputValidator.ref, return the issues (and whatever the runner needs), return null/no-op when the Play declares no outputValidator, and throw on an unknown ref — exactly the existing behavior. Export it through packages/core/src/index.ts. Keep it additive and non-breaking.

(2) REWRITE Clause C to prove BOTH directions through that REAL field-driven validation path (the one selected by the declared validators/founder-closeout ref), WITHOUT depending on the LLM to misbehave:
   - VALID PATH (keep the real-LLM, runner-owned proof that already passed): drive the REAL runner wrap-up path (runRun) so the runner — not a persona choice — invokes the wrap-up Play with a REAL LLM invocation, producing a well-formed closeout, and the runner validates it through the declared validators/founder-closeout ref → status completed, zero validation issues, llmInvoked true. This proves mandatory-trigger-without-persona-discretion + validation-passes-via-declared-ref end to end with a real LLM. (You may keep using the existing approach of instructing the model to emit the well-formed closeout; that direction works.)
   - REJECTION PATH (make it deterministic, no second LLM call): load the real base wrap-up Play (loadPlay), and call the NEW exported validation function directly with a KNOWN-malformed closeout string (e.g. 'PLAY CLOSEOUT'). Assert it returns issues (rejected). Also assert the well-formed closeout string returns ZERO issues, and that selection is genuinely driven by the declared ref: a Play with no outputValidator declared returns null/no validation, and an unknown ref throws. This proves the declared outputValidator contract HAS TEETH and is wired to the field — through the exact production function the runner uses — without relying on a model to produce garbage on command. Drop the wasteful second real-LLM call the old malformed case made.

Clause C PASSES only if: real-LLM valid wrap-up completes and validates clean via the declared ref; the exported validator flags the known-malformed string; the exported validator passes the well-formed string; ref-driven selection (none → no validation; unknown → throw) holds; and the exported function is the SAME one the runner uses (no duplicated contract).

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: clauses A and B stay green and unchanged; the 8a foundation behavior (convention, validator wiring, hybrid Play) is unchanged except the additive export + the runner refactor to use it; all existing tests still pass; no weakened tests; one-level dispatch boundary stands.

WRITE-SCOPE: scripts/proof-hybrid-play.mjs (primary), packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts and packages/core/src/index.ts (the additive export + one-owner refactor only). Do not touch personas, priorities, or unrelated logic.

EXIT CRITERION (run these and report exit codes + FULL captured output, including the real LLM transcript for the valid wrap-up and the deterministic rejection evidence):
- node scripts/proof-hybrid-play.mjs  (must now exit 0 with EVERY clause PASS — A, B, C — using the real codex adapter; paste the full clause table and captured outputs)
- pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test  (all green)
- pnpm --filter @cocoder/core exec tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json  (clean)
Report the full diff, the three commands' exit codes, and confirm explicitly that (a) the valid wrap-up used a real LLM invocation through the runner, (b) the rejection path exercises the same exported validator the runner uses, and (c) clauses A and B are unchanged. Do NOT run git. If the real adapter is genuinely unavailable, STOP and report that precisely rather than mocking.
